eyebrow: C4 Dynamic · runtime flow — Login + MFA + token issue
title: Execution order: how the components collaborate
subtitle: numbered steps = runtime order · each step = component → component + protocol · complements the api-auth L3 (static)

steps.detail: credentials submitted via api-gateway; router dispatches to the Login Controller.
password / WebAuthn verified against the credential store.
credential OK → check whether MFA is required for this user/org.
OTP issued; SMS path: api-challenge → [Kafka] → sms-processor → [HTTPS] AWS SNS.
user enters the code; orchestrator verifies it via api-challenge ✓.
builds access + ID tokens for the authenticated subject.
JWT signed by the key holder — the signing key never enters api-auth.
session persisted (KeyDB via backend); secure cookie prepared.
login complete — tokens returned, session cookie set.

relsTitle: This is a C4 Dynamic view (runtime order) — it complements the static L3 component views.
rels: L3 tells you which components exist & what depends on what
Dynamic tells you in what order they run for one request
refs: runs across: api-auth (L3, §A2) · backend-auth (L3, §A6) · api-challenge · api-oauth2 (L3, §A3) — zoom out: Container view Auth & Tokens (L2)
caption: Identity Platform · C4 Dynamic · Login + MFA + token issue — step order across api-auth components
